task_completion.description: Open the settings or options dialog in bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application is open and its settings/options window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enable the global hotkey feature and set the trigger key to 'F10'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the bcWebCam settings dialog, a checkbox or toggle for a global hotkey is checked/enabled, and the assigned key is explicitly set to 'F10'.

task_completion.description: Change the application behavior after a successful scan to end reading and minimize the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the settings dialog, an option related to post-scan behavior (e.g., 'after successful scan') is selected to 'minimize' or 'end reading and minimize'.

task_completion.description: Take a screenshot of the configured settings dialog and save it to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\hotkey_configured.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool (like Snipping Tool, Paint, or a terminal command) is visible saving an image of the settings dialog, with the file path explicitly showing 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\hotkey_configured.png'.

task_completion.description: Apply or save the configured settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'OK', 'Save', or 'Apply' button in the settings dialog, or the dialog is seen closing after the settings were configured.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam GUI to configure settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual bcWebCam application interface being clicked and modified, rather than editing a configuration file in a text editor or using terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually captured and saved the screenshot of the settings dialog.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot file is seen being saved through a legitimate tool (e.g., Paint, Snipping Tool) with the correct path, rather than just creating a dummy file or empty text file named .png.

integrity.description: The screenshot was taken while the settings dialog was open and properly configured.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being saved in the screenshot tool clearly shows the bcWebCam settings dialog with 'F10' and 'minimize' options selected.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected hotkey to be configured is 'F10'. The expected post-scan action is 'minimize'. The screenshot must be saved exactly to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\hotkey_configured.png'.
